title: Choose the Right ML Algorithm
tagline: Given 5 business problems, pick the right algorithm and explain why.
scenario: A data team lead interviews candidates by giving them 5 real business problems and asking which machine learning approach (classification, regression, clustering, etc.) fits each one. The goal is not to code — it is to think.
brief: For each of the 5 problems below, identify the ML task type, suggest a specific algorithm, and explain why it is a better fit than the alternatives.
deliverables: Problem 1: Predict whether a loan application will default (yes/no) — your approach
Problem 2: Estimate the sale price of a house — your approach
Problem 3: Group customers into segments based on purchasing behaviour — your approach
Problem 4: Detect fraudulent credit card transactions in real-time — your approach
Problem 5: Recommend products to users based on their browsing history — your approach
submissionGuidance: For each problem, state: (1) the ML task type, (2) a specific algorithm, (3) why you chose it over one alternative. "Use deep learning" is not a valid answer without justification.

rubric.label: Task Classification
rubric.description: Is each problem correctly classified as regression, classification, clustering, etc.?

rubric.label: Algorithm Selection
rubric.description: Are the chosen algorithms appropriate and practically useful?

rubric.label: Reasoning Quality
rubric.description: Are the trade-off explanations specific rather than generic?
